description: Create shared helpers: _publish_error_safely, _create_device_store. Make add_telemetry/add_device/add_command accept is_root. Prep for subsequent phases.
title: Extract cross-cutting helpers

description: Unify root/named registration paths. Extract _validate_telemetry_params. Collapse decorator body to single delegation.
title: Refactor App.telemetry() (score 20→<15)

description: Extract _init_telemetry_handler and _execute_telemetry_cycle. Flatten while→try→if nesting.
title: Refactor App._run_telemetry() (score 17→<15)

description: Decompose into _register_device_proxy, _init_command_store, _init_command_handler, _register_command_proxy. Reduce from 32 to ~8-10.
title: Refactor App._wire_router() (score 32→<15)

description: Extract _validate_log_options, _apply_cli_overrides, _run_with_error_handling from nested callback.
title: Refactor build_cli() (score 18→<15)

description: Remove all # noqa: CCR001 comments. Verify task complexity passes clean. Full task pre-pr.
title: Remove noqa suppressions and verify

description: Bundle of medium/low code quality improvements: reduce _connection_loop complexity, unify composite patterns, add eval() safety comment, replace assert with RuntimeError, centralize bool-guard validation, relocate _import_string.
title: Code quality housekeeping

description: Extract _build_will() and _extract_password() from _connection_loop (complexity 13) in _mqtt.py L359.
title: Extract _connection_loop helpers in _mqtt.py

close_reason: Audited Strategy and SavePolicy composites. Init flattening is identical but core methods diverge: different signatures (should_publish vs should_save), different evaluation semantics (eager vs lazy), and Strategy has extra methods (_bind, on_published). Generic base would only unify __init__ (~16 lines saved) while adding Generic[T] complexity. Not worth the abstraction.
description: AnyStrategy/AllStrategy and AnySavePolicy/AllSavePolicy are structurally identical copy-paste. Introduce generic _CompositeBase[T] or abstract mixin.
title: Unify Strategy/SavePolicy composite pattern

description: Add inline safety comment to eval() at _injection.py L100 explaining why it's safe (evaluates function's own type annotation, not user input).
title: Add safety rationale for eval() in _injection.py

description: Replace assert callable(raw_impl) at L829 and assert self._store at L926 with explicit RuntimeError raises. Asserts are stripped with python -O.
title: Replace assert with RuntimeError in _app.py

close_reason: Audited 6 bool-guard locations across _filters.py and _strategies.py. Patterns diverge too much (raise vs return, different msg formats, dict-iteration vs scalar). Centralization would save ~6 lines for 3 calls while adding indirection. Not worth the abstraction.
description: Extract shared _validate_numeric() helper to replace repeated isinstance(val, bool) checks in Pt1Filter, MedianFilter, OneEuroFilter, OnChange constructors.
title: Centralize bool-guard validation in filters/strategies

description: _import_string lives in _context.py but is called from _app.py and _registration.py. Move to a _utils.py module.
title: Relocate _import_string to utility module

description: Extract responsibility clusters from the 2,086-line App class (maintainability index B/10.25, 58+ methods) into focused modules. The App class becomes a thin facade delegating to extracted collaborators. Target: all source files rated A by radon MI.
title: Decompose _app.py God class

description: Remove unused _startup_hooks/_shutdown_hooks. Extract shared runner helpers (_create_device_store, _save_store_on_shutdown, _publish_error_safely, _maybe_persist) to _runner_utils.py.
title: Dead code cleanup + shared runner utils

description: Move _check_device_name, _colliding_names, _validate_name_unique, _validate_single_root, _warn_if_mixing to _registration.py as free functions. ~85 lines removed from App.
title: Name validation → _registration.py

description: Extract TelemetryRunner class to _telemetry_runner.py (~396 lines). Includes _GroupState, _to_ms, and 17 execution/scheduling methods. App delegates via TelemetryRunner(store=...) in _run_async.
title: Telemetry runner extraction

description: Extract CommandRunner class to _command_runner.py (~161 lines). Owns _command_init_results and _command_stores exclusively. 6 methods including _run_command, _register_command_proxy, _register_device_proxy.
title: Command runner extraction

description: Slim _run_async to recipe-style delegation. Extract adapter lifecycle and wiring helpers. Target: _app.py <800 lines, all source files MI grade A.
title: Facade polish + final metrics

description: Add testcontainers-python to dev dependencies. Add Mosquitto-based integration tests using ephemeral broker container. Test: connect/disconnect, publish/subscribe round-trip, LWT delivery, reconnection after broker restart. Create conftest.py fixture for broker lifecycle. Add task test:mqtt to Taskfile.yml. Mark tests with @pytest.mark.integration. Learning: testcontainers pattern, ephemeral infrastructure as code.
title: P6.2: MQTT integration tests (testcontainers)

description: Add init= to all three decorators. Factory runs once, result injected by type.
title: init= callback on decorators

description: Replace the 10-element tuple return from _init_group_handlers with a @dataclass (_GroupState). Currently destructured into 10 positional variables at the call site — fragile and opaque. Quick win, low risk.
title: Replace _init_group_handlers tuple with GroupState dataclass

description: Phase 1: Core store protocol and 4 backend implementations with full test coverage.
title: Store protocol + backends (JsonFileStore, SqliteStore, MemoryStore, NullStore)

description: Phase 2 of supply chain security. Builds on ADR-017 (CycloneDX SBOM via syft). Covers: PyPI attestations (PEP 740 / SLSA provenance), devcontainer image SBOM, GitHub Dependency Submission API integration, and VEX workflow evaluation.
title: Epic: Supply chain security Phase 2

description: Add --show-devices as is_eager=True option in _cli.py. Calls build_registry_snapshot() and renders a formatted table (plain text, tabular). Also add --show-devices-json for machine-readable JSON output. Short-circuits before settings validation (like --version). Acceptance: both flags work, output is accurate, tested.
title: P1.3: --show-devices CLI flag

description: Add introspection capabilities to the framework: a machine-readable device registry (JSON output) and a human-readable --show-devices CLI flag. Both share the same underlying data (registration snapshot). Depends on Epic 1 (clean internals).
title: Epic: Device Introspection (P1.3 + P3.1)

description: Establish performance baselines before Rust work. Add pytest-benchmark to dev dependencies. Create tests/benchmarks/ directory. Benchmarks: Pt1Filter.update() throughput at 100Hz/1kHz/10kHz simulated rates, MedianFilter.update() with window sizes 3/5/11, OneEuroFilter.update() with typical params, orjson.dumps() for typical telemetry payloads (3-10 fields). Target both dev machine and RPi Zero. Acceptance: benchmark suite runs, baseline JSON output saved.
title: P4.3: Benchmark suite for hot paths

description:
Two related vulnerability disclosure improvements:

1. **Private Vulnerability Reporting (4a):** Enable GitHub's private vulnerability reporting feature in repo settings (Security → Advisories). Lets security researchers report issues privately instead of via public issues. Creates a structured intake form linked from SECURITY.md. Standard practice for published PyPI packages.

2. **Security Policy (4b):** Create SECURITY.md documenting how to report vulnerabilities, supported versions, and expected response timeline. Required companion to private reporting. PyPI ecosystem convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- SECURITY.md committed to repo root with clear reporting instructions
- Private vulnerability reporting enabled in repo settings
- security policy link visible on repo sidebar
title: Add SECURITY.md and enable private vulnerability reporting

description: Update cosalette/filters.py to auto-select Rust implementations when cosalette-filters-rs is installed, with transparent fallback to Python. Add cosalette[fast-filters] extra to pyproject.toml. User code does not change: 'from cosalette.filters import Pt1Filter' picks the fastest available. Learning: feature detection via import, optional dependencies, extras_require. Acceptance: import works with and without Rust package.
title: P4.1d: Python fallback import for Rust filters

description: Phase 2: DeviceStore dict-like wrapper with dirty tracking, DI injection into handlers, auto-load/save in telemetry and device loops.
title: DeviceStore wrapper + DI integration + framework loop integration

description: Gate task for deferred decision T1 (docs/TODO/T1-metrics-port.md). Evaluate MetricsPort protocol design when mqtt2prometheus bridge validates the need for framework-internal metrics. References ADR-006 (hexagonal), ADR-012 (health reporting), ADR-016 (adapter lifecycle).
title: Evaluate MetricsPort protocol for observability

description: Phase 1: Add group parameter to telemetry registration layer and public API. 1) Add group: str | None = None to _TelemetryRegistration dataclass. 2) Add group param to telemetry() decorator, thread to both paths (named and root). 3) Add group param to add_telemetry() imperative method. 4) Validation: reject empty string group=''. 5) Tests: verify group field stored, validation, backward compat. Acceptance: All existing tests pass. New tests verify group field.
title: Data model + API surface (group= parameter)

description: Add a Mermaid flowchart to docs/concepts/device-archetypes.md with the decision algorithm: Does it poll periodically? -> Commands? -> Shared loop? -> Archetype recommendation. Reference ADR-019 (scoped uniqueness) for the telemetry+command pairing path. Include examples for each leaf node.
title: P7.2: Device type decision tree

description: Add enabled= parameter (bool or callable) to all device decorators and imperative add_* methods. When enabled=False (or callable returns False), the device is skipped at registration time. Cleans up imperative if/else blocks around device registration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4
title: Epic 4: Conditional Device Registration

description: Add enabled= parameter (bool | Callable[[], bool]) to device(), telemetry(), command() decorators and their add_* imperative counterparts. When enabled=False or callable returns False, skip registration entirely (decorator returns the function unmodified, add_* is a no-op). Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §4
title: Add enabled= parameter to device decorators

description: Tests for: bool True/False, callable returning True/False, disabled device not started/not in registry, enabled device works normally, works with all three device types, works with both decorator and imperative registration.
title: Add tests for conditional device registration

description: Update docs/guides/telemetry-device.md, docs/guides/command-device.md, docs/reference/api.md to document enabled= parameter. Show before/after examples of conditional registration.
title: Update docs for conditional device registration

description: Add init field to registration dataclasses, init= param to decorators with fail-fast validation, init-calling logic in _run_telemetry/_run_device/_run_command. Guard against type collisions with KNOWN_INJECTABLE_TYPES.
title: Core init= on telemetry, device, command

description: If a lifecycle adapter __aenter__ hangs indefinitely, setting shutdown_event cannot interrupt it (no cancellation). The process appears stuck. Consider wrapping adapter entry in asyncio.wait_for with a configurable timeout, or using a cancel scope. Filed from COS-rt0 fix — the signal handler timing gap is closed, but a truly hanging __aenter__ remains uninterruptible.
title: Adapter entry timeout/cancellation during shutdown

close_reason: Framework reference rewritten for 0.1.8 (local instruction file)
title: Rewrite cosalette-framework-reference.instruction.md for 0.1.8
Scoped name uniqueness: allow telemetry+command to share MQTT topic namespace
ADR-020: Deferred interval resolution design decision

description: Create cosalette-filters-rs/ directory with: Cargo.toml (pyo3 dependency), pyproject.toml (maturin build backend), src/lib.rs (pyo3 module definition with empty #[pymodule]). Builds an empty Python extension module. Learning: Rust project structure, Cargo.toml, pyo3 module definition, maturin config. Acceptance: 'maturin develop' builds, 'import cosalette_filters_rs' works.
title: P4.1a: Rust project scaffold (pyo3/maturin)

description: Implement coalescing groups in cosalette so that telemetry handlers sharing a group name execute together at coinciding tick boundaries, sharing one adapter session window. Per ADR-007/ADR-018, the user declares grouping via an explicit group= parameter on the registration API. Ref: docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md, docs/planning/telemetry-coalescing-groups.md, docs/planning/telemetry-session-coalescing.md
title: Epic: Telemetry Coalescing Groups
Extend ClockPort with async sleep method

description: GitHub Actions workflow for cross-platform wheel building: manylinux (x86_64, aarch64), musllinux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS (x86_64, arm64). ARM cross-compilation for RPi Zero targets using cargo-zigbuild. Learning: cross-compilation, maturin CI, cargo-zigbuild for ARM. Acceptance: wheels build in CI for all target platforms.
title: P4.1e: CI wheel matrix for Rust filters

description: Phase 2: Implement the tick-aligned priority-queue scheduler. 1) Create _run_telemetry_group() method with heapq-based scheduler. 2) Integer-millisecond tick arithmetic (_to_ms helper). 3) Per-handler DI resolution, init functions, publish strategies. 4) Per-handler error isolation within batches. 5) Init failure excludes handler from group. 6) Shutdown-aware sleep using first handler DeviceContext. 7) Device store save on shutdown (finally block). 8) Update _start_device_tasks() to partition by group. 9) Unit tests: single handler, same interval, mixed intervals, error isolation, init failure, shutdown, registration order. Acceptance: Grouped handlers coalesce correctly. Ungrouped unchanged.
title: Tick-aligned group scheduler (_run_telemetry_group)

description: Both functions do signature-based DI invocation with slightly different semantics. Evaluate creating _invoke_with_injection(func, plan, providers) in _injection.py. Key differences: _call_init uses pre-computed plan + async validation + result-type shadow check; _call_factory builds plan at call time + zero-arg fast path. Unify if clean, close as won't-do with documented rationale if forced unification adds complexity. Depends on P5.3 (adapter relocation clarifies ownership).
title: P5.5: Evaluate unifying _call_factory/_call_init

description: Implement Pt1Filter as #[pyclass] with update(), value getter, reset(). Validate against existing Python test suite + Hypothesis property tests. Parametrize tests to run against both Python and Rust backends. Learning: #[pyclass], #[pymethods], #[getter], Python<->Rust type mapping, PyResult/PyValueError. Acceptance: all existing Pt1Filter tests pass for both backends.
title: P4.1b: Pt1Filter in Rust

description: Split the 3,513-line test_app.py into focused test files by responsibility (lifecycle, injection, init, etc.). Mirrors the _app.py decomposition. TestRunAsync alone is ~1,700 lines.
title: Split test_app.py monolith

description: Add OnTrigger publish strategy — a distinct strategy that publishes only when the return value changes (beyond optional threshold). Conceptually different from OnChange: OnTrigger is about event detection (something happened), OnChange is about value drift (state changed). Implementation may share internals with OnChange, but the contract and name are distinct. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §6
title: Epic 6: Event-Driven Telemetry (OnTrigger)

description: New PublishStrategy in _strategies.py. OnTrigger publishes when return dict values cross a trigger condition (change from previous). Supports optional per-field thresholds. Distinct contract from OnChange: OnTrigger = event detection (something happened), OnChange = value drift (state drifted). May share internal mechanics with OnChange but public API and naming are distinct. Composes with existing strategies via | and &.
title: Design and implement OnTrigger strategy

description: Tests for: trigger on change, threshold suppression, composition with Every via | and &, stateful counter advancement (eager eval), per-field thresholds, None handling, first-value behavior.
title: Add tests for OnTrigger strategy

description: Update docs/concepts/publish-strategies.md (add OnTrigger with clear OnChange vs OnTrigger distinction), docs/reference/api.md (new strategy in public API). Consider ADR update if warranted (ADR-013 covers publish strategies).
title: Update docs for OnTrigger strategy

close_reason: Already addressed in a previous session
title: File structure refactoring (_app.py + test_app.py splits)

description: Create a .github/instructions/cosalette.instructions.md template file that adopter projects (gas2mqtt, vito2mqtt, etc.) copy and customize. Template includes: project-specific device registry (names, types, intervals), adapter list, settings schema, testing patterns (AppHarness, MockMqttClient), CLI usage. Reference P3.1 introspection output (--show-devices-json) as a way to auto-generate parts.
title: P3.4: Instruction file template for adopter projects

description: Add a Mermaid diagram to docs/concepts/architecture.md showing hexagonal layers: User Code -> App (composition root) -> Ports (MqttPort, ClockPort, Filter, Store) -> Adapters -> Infrastructure. Include the bootstrap lifecycle phases (Bootstrap -> Wire -> Run -> Teardown). Show the injection system connecting handlers to ports.
title: P7.1: Architecture overview diagram

description: Relax global name uniqueness to per-type uniqueness. Allow telemetry+command pairs to share names. Reject device+telemetry or device+command same-name pairs. Add comprehensive tests for all collision scenarios.
title: Scope _validate_name_unique per registration type

description: Evaluated and deferred. VEX is fundamentally manual triage — premature for a v0.1.x project with 4 direct dependencies. CycloneDX natively supports VEX when needed later. No architectural changes required to add it in the future.
title: Evaluate VEX workflow

description: Auto-manage adapters implementing __aenter__/__aexit__ (context manager protocol). Framework calls lifecycle methods during startup/shutdown automatically — no explicit lifespan= hook needed for the common case. Only context manager protocol supported (not named methods). Lifespan= hook remains for advanced orchestration. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1
title: Epic 1: Adapter Lifecycle Protocol

description: Add AdapterLifecycle protocol detection to the framework. Implement _is_lifecycle_adapter() helper that checks if a resolved adapter instance supports __aenter__/__aexit__. This is purely the protocol definition and detection — no orchestration changes yet. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1
title: Define adapter lifecycle protocol (context manager)

description: Modify _run_async Phase 1 (adapter resolution) and Phase 3/4 (startup/shutdown) to auto-enter/exit lifecycle adapters via async context manager protocol. Auto-managed adapters enter BEFORE the user lifespan= hook and exit AFTER it. Handle errors during __aenter__/__aexit__ gracefully. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1
title: Implement auto-lifecycle in _run_async

description: Unit tests for: protocol detection (_is_lifecycle_adapter), auto-init/close via context manager, adapters that are NOT lifecycle (passthrough), mixed adapters (some lifecycle some not), coexistence with lifespan= hook, error handling during __aenter__/__aexit__, ordering guarantees (adapters before lifespan). Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1
title: Add tests for adapter lifecycle protocol

description: Create ADR-016 for adapter lifecycle protocol decision. Update docs/guides/adapters.md, docs/guides/lifespan.md, docs/concepts/lifecycle.md, docs/reference/api.md to document auto-lifecycle behavior, context manager convention, and coexistence with lifespan= hook. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §1
title: Update docs for adapter lifecycle protocol

description: Phase 4: ADR, concept page, telemetry device guide section, testing guide.
title: Persistence documentation (ADR-015, concept doc, guide updates)

description: Submit the CycloneDX SBOM to GitHub's Dependency Submission API so cosalette's dependency graph appears in the GitHub UI (Insights → Dependency graph). Uses anchore/sbom-action with upload-dependency-snapshot or a dedicated submission step. Enables Dependabot alerts for transitive dependencies.
title: GitHub Dependency Submission API integration

description: Write ADR-019 documenting the decision to relax global name uniqueness. Update concept docs, guide docs, and reference docs to reflect the new behavior.
title: ADR-019 and documentation for scoped name uniqueness

description: Unify single-telemetry (_process_telemetry_result) and group-telemetry (_process_group_handler_result) behind a shared handler. Both implement the same publish → persist → error-clear → error-handle pipeline. Also extract _run_telemetry_group helpers to reduce its complexity from 14.
title: Unify telemetry processing paths

description: Create cosalette._introspect module with build_registry_snapshot(app) -> dict. Produces structured dict of all devices, commands, telemetry, adapters with metadata. Handle unresolved intervals (show '<deferred>'), callable inits (true/false), strategy repr(). JSON-serializable output. Acceptance: snapshot covers all registration types, round-trips through json.dumps(), tested.
title: P3.1: Introspection module (_introspect.py)

description: Add orjson>=3.10 to pyproject.toml dependencies. Create cosalette._json module with dumps() and loads() wrappers. dumps() returns str (decodes orjson bytes output). Replace all json.dumps/json.loads across: _context.py L135 (publish_state), _errors.py L71 (ErrorPayload.to_json), _health.py L101 (heartbeat), _logging.py L89 (JsonFormatter), _stores.py L147/L175/L194/L238/L244. Handle orjson differences: OPT_INDENT_2 for stores, default= parameter semantics. Write ADR for the decision. Acceptance: all tests pass, verified on Python 3.14.
title: P4.2: Adopt orjson as hard dependency

description: Add syft binary to the devcontainer Dockerfile so it's available for local SBOM generation. Use COPY --from=docker.io/anchore/syft:latest approach.
title: Install syft in devcontainer

description: Add build_injection_plan() at app.adapter() registration time for factory callables. Fail-fast on bad signatures.
title: Adapter factory fail-fast validation
Deferred interval resolution — IntervalSpec type + resolution in _run_async

description: Final review after all 6 feature epics land. Goal: ensure everything reads as if designed together from the beginning — not as 6 balconies bolted on. Covers source code consistency, public API coherence, documentation narrative flow, ADR cross-references, and test suite organization. Depends on all other epics. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md (all sections)
title: Epic 7: Documentation & Code Consistency Review

description: Review _app.py, _injection.py, _strategies.py, and any new modules added by Epics 1-6. Ensure consistent naming conventions, parameter ordering, error message style, docstring format. Refactor any inconsistencies so the code reads as one cohesive design.
title: Source code consistency audit

description: Review __init__.py exports. Ensure all new types (OnTrigger, add_device, add_telemetry, add_command, adapters= parameter, enabled= etc.) are properly exported. Verify the public API has a coherent shape — naming, grouping, discoverability.
title: Public API audit (__init__.py exports)

description: Review ALL guides, concepts, and reference docs. Ensure the 6 features are woven into the narrative naturally — not as appendices or addenda. Restructure any docs that feel like afterthoughts. The reader should not be able to tell these features were added later. Check cross-references, examples, and conceptual flow.
title: Documentation narrative audit

description: Review all ADRs (001-016+). Ensure cross-references are correct, the decision trail makes sense read sequentially, and any new ADRs from Epics 1-6 are properly linked. Update ADR index.
title: ADR consistency audit

description: Review test organization across all tests added in Epics 1-6. Ensure: tests mirror source structure, shared fixtures are used where possible, naming conventions are uniform, no duplicated test setup, markers used consistently.
title: Test suite audit

description: Phase 3: Polish and documentation. 1) Float precision tests (300x12 == 3600 coalesces). 2) Integration tests with AppHarness + FakeClock (grouped+ungrouped coexist, multiple groups, full MQTT verification). 3) Write ADR-018-coalescing-groups.md (promote from planning docs). 4) Update concept docs (architecture, telemetry). 5) Update guides (telemetry-device, adapters). 6) Update API reference. 7) Delete planning docs (coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md, telemetry-coalescing-groups.md, telemetry-session-coalescing.md) after ADR is written. Acceptance: Full test coverage, docs updated, ADR-018 in place.
title: Edge cases, integration tests + ADR-018

description: Add adapters= dict parameter to App.__init__() for grouped, constructor-level adapter declaration. Accepts dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]]. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls. Benefits from Epic 2's settings-aware DI for the adapter values. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §5
title: Epic 5: Declarative Adapter Block

description: Accept adapters= dict[type, tuple[impl, dry_run_impl]] in App(). Parse tuples and register via existing adapter() internals. Support both tuple (impl, dry_run) and bare impl value. Coexists with imperative app.adapter() calls — duplicates raise ValueError.
title: Add adapters= constructor parameter

description: Tests for: dict registration, bare impl (no dry_run), mixed with imperative app.adapter(), duplicate port detection, empty dict, type checking.
title: Add tests for declarative adapter block

description: Update docs/guides/adapters.md (show adapters= dict pattern), docs/reference/api.md (updated App constructor signature).
title: Update docs for declarative adapter block

description: Generate a CycloneDX SBOM for the devcontainer image (ghcr.io/ff-fab/cosalette-devcontainer). Run syft against the built image in the devcontainer build workflow and attach as a release asset or publish to GHCR alongside the image. Gives contributors transparency into the dev toolchain dependencies.
title: DevContainer image SBOM

description: Add imperative app.add_device(), app.add_telemetry(), app.add_command() methods for registering imported functions without pass-through wrappers. Refactor decorators to call these internally (DRY). Mirrors FastAPI's app.add_api_route() pattern. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3
title: Epic 3: Direct Function Registration

description: Implement add_device(name, func, *, init=), add_telemetry(name, func, *, interval=, publish=, persist=, init=), add_command(name, func, *, init=) on App class. Refactor existing device(), telemetry(), command() decorators to call these internally for DRY. All validation/registration logic moves to the add_* methods; decorators become thin wrappers. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §3
title: Add imperative registration methods

description: Tests for: imperative registration works, equivalence with decorator registration, name collision detection works across both, mixed imperative + decorator usage, all three device types (device/telemetry/command).
title: Add tests for direct function registration

description: Update docs/guides/telemetry-device.md, docs/guides/command-device.md, docs/reference/api.md to document add_device/add_telemetry/add_command methods. Show both decorator and imperative patterns side by side.
title: Update docs for direct function registration

description: Fix inconsistencies in the public API surface: missing composite exports, missing strategies.py re-export module.
title: API surface consistency

description: Add AnyStrategy and AllStrategy to cosalette.__all__. Users composing strategies with |/& get these types but can't import them for type annotations. Inconsistent with persist.py which exports composites.
title: Export AnyStrategy/AllStrategy in __init__.py

description: Create cosalette/strategies.py re-export module for Every, OnChange, AnyStrategy, AllStrategy. Follows the pattern of stores.py, filters.py, persist.py.
title: Add strategies.py re-export module

description: Strengthen testing with property-based tests (Hypothesis) for strategies and filters, and integration tests against a real MQTT broker (Mosquitto via testcontainers-python). Both are learning opportunities.
title: Epic: Testing Upgrades (P6.1 + P6.2)

close_reason: Migration prompt rewritten for 0.1.8 (local instruction file)
title: Rewrite cosalette-migration-prompt.md for 0.1.8

description: Phase 3: persist= parameter with composable save policies mirroring publish= pattern.
title: Save policies (SaveOnPublish, SaveOnChange, SaveOnShutdown) + composition

description: Create docs/reference/errors.md cataloging all framework-raised exceptions: registration-time errors (TypeError for missing annotations/async init/bool params, ValueError for duplicate names/negative intervals), runtime errors (RuntimeError for settings access before bootstrap/MQTT not connected), error publishing (ErrorPayload schema, topic layout {prefix}/error and {prefix}/{device}/error, error_type_map pattern). Include causes, example messages, and fixes for each.
title: P3.2: Error taxonomy reference page

description: Move MqttClient class (~260 lines, production adapter with reconnection loop) from _mqtt.py into new _mqtt_client.py. Keep protocols (MqttPort, MqttLifecycle, MqttMessageHandler), WillConfig, NullMqttClient, MockMqttClient, MessageCallback in _mqtt.py. Re-export MqttClient from _mqtt.py for import compatibility. Update _wiring.py import. Acceptance: all tests pass, no import changes needed in user-facing code.
title: P5.1: Extract MqttClient from _mqtt.py

description: Eliminate cast(float, reg.interval) in _telemetry_runner.py (lines 178 and 391). After resolve_intervals() runs in _wiring.py, all callable intervals become floats, but the type still says IntervalSpec. Introduce _ResolvedTelemetryRegistration frozen dataclass with interval: float (or add a resolved_interval: float field). resolve_intervals() returns the resolved type. Remove both cast() calls. Acceptance: pyright/mypy clean, no cast() for intervals.
title: P5.4: Resolved interval type safety

description: Refactor release-please.yml to build the wheel once and upload the same artifact to both TestPyPI and PyPI, instead of building independently in each job.
title: Fix double-build in release workflow

description: Document the decision to use syft + CycloneDX for SBOM generation at release time. Cover: format choice (CycloneDX over SPDX), generator choice (syft), integration points (release workflow, devcontainer, Taskfile), what's deferred (attestations, SPDX, devcontainer image SBOM).
title: Write ADR-017: SBOM generation

description: Extend adapter resolution to inspect __init__ type hints and auto-inject Settings (DI). Eliminates factory function boilerplate — adapter classes that type-hint Settings in __init__ get it injected automatically. Not backwards compatible. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2
title: Epic 2: Settings-Aware Adapter Constructors

description: Modify _resolve_adapters / _call_factory in _app.py to inspect __init__ signatures of adapter classes and auto-inject Settings (and potentially other registered types like ClockPort). Reuse the same signature-based DI pattern from _injection.py. When a class has __init__(self, settings: MySettings), resolve Settings and inject. No-arg constructors remain supported. Factory callables continue to work as before. Ref: docs/TODO/main-declarativeness-improvements.md §2
title: Extend adapter resolution for DI

description: Tests for: Settings-injected constructors, fallback to no-arg, factory functions still work, adapter with wrong/missing type hint fails clearly, Settings subclass injection, multiple injectable params.
title: Add tests for settings-aware adapter DI

description: Update docs/guides/adapters.md (show DI-based registration replacing factory functions), docs/concepts/configuration.md (explain adapter DI resolution), docs/reference/api.md (updated adapter() signature docs).
title: Update docs for settings-aware adapter DI

description: Create cosalette-filters-rs Rust crate exposing Pt1Filter, MedianFilter, OneEuroFilter as Python classes via pyo3/maturin. Starts with a benchmark suite (P4.3) to establish baselines. Pure learning project with practical value for RPi Zero hardware. Same Filter protocol as Python implementations — transparent fallback.
title: Epic: Rust Signal Filters (P4.1 + P4.3)

description: Add hypothesis to dev dependencies. Write property-based tests for signal filters and publish strategies. Target files: tests/unit/test_filters_properties.py and tests/unit/test_strategies_properties.py. Properties: Pt1Filter output converges to constant input, MedianFilter output bounded by window min/max, OnChange(threshold=T) never publishes when delta < T, Every(seconds=N) rate-limits correctly. Learning: property-based testing philosophy, @given, @settings, shrinking.
title: P6.1: Property-based tests (Hypothesis)

description: Add a 'sbom' task that builds the wheel and generates a CycloneDX JSON SBOM from the dist/ directory using syft.
title: Add task sbom to Taskfile

description: _run_telemetry_group computes elapsed time from sleep_ctx.clock.now() (injected ClockPort) but ctx.sleep() uses asyncio.sleep (real monotonic time). With FakeClock, clock.now() never advances during sleeps so elapsed is always 0, causing wait_seconds to be an absolute offset rather than a delta. This is harmless in current tests (short intervals, shutdown after 1-2 ticks) but would break multi-tick deterministic test scenarios. Fix: either advance FakeClock inside ctx.sleep(), or base elapsed calculation on time.monotonic()/loop.time(). See PR #62 review comments from Copilot.
title: FakeClock time-source mismatch in group scheduler

description: Phase 5: Update gas2mqtt example to use cosalette.stores instead of its own implementation.
title: Migrate gas2mqtt example to framework persistence

description: Signal handlers are installed after lifecycle adapters are entered in _run_async(). If SIGTERM/SIGINT arrives during a slow __aenter__, Python's default handler terminates the process without setting shutdown_event and without running adapter/health/MQTT cleanup. Moving _install_signal_handlers() before the async with _enter_lifecycle_adapters() block would close this window, but requires design thought about what happens if shutdown fires during adapter entry (abort remaining entries?). Ref: PR #49 review feedback.
title: Signal handler timing gap: install before adapter lifecycle entry

description: Move _AdapterEntry dataclass, _call_factory(), _is_async_context_manager(), and _build_adapter_providers() from _registration.py to _adapter_lifecycle.py. This colocates all adapter-related code: registration types + resolution + lifecycle. Update imports in _app.py (currently imports _AdapterEntry from _registration). Acceptance: all tests pass, clean module boundaries.
title: P5.3: Relocate _AdapterEntry to _adapter_lifecycle.py

description:
Two related security scanning improvements:

1. **CodeQL Code Scanning (2a):** Add .github/workflows/codeql.yml for Python static security analysis. Runs on push to main, PRs, and weekly schedule. Catches unsanitized data patterns, command injection, path traversal — relevant since cosalette processes external MQTT messages and device responses. Free for public repos.

2. **Secret Scanning: Validity Checks (2b):** Enable in repo settings. Verifies detected secrets are actually live/active (reduces false positives from test values). Settings toggle only.

Acceptance criteria:
- codeql.yml workflow added and passing on main
- Secret validity checks enabled in repo settings
- No new CodeQL alerts (or all triaged)
title: Add CodeQL scanning workflow and enable secret validity checks

description: Prevent _publish_all_available from publishing duplicate availability for shared names. Ensure log messages in _run_telemetry and _run_command include archetype qualifier when names overlap.
title: Deduplicate health reporting and qualify log messages

description: Stabilize and clean up the codebase before adding new features. Extract MqttClient from _mqtt.py, relocate _AdapterEntry, evaluate _call_factory/_call_init unification, add resolved interval type safety, remove dead delegate methods on App. No backward compat constraint (v0.1.x, no production).
title: Epic: Internal Refactoring (P5.x)

description: Replace stdlib json with orjson (Rust-based, 5-10x faster) as a hard dependency. Create cosalette._json module centralizing serialization. Affects 5 json.dumps call sites: _context.py (publish_state hot path), _errors.py, _health.py, _logging.py, _stores.py. orjson.dumps() returns bytes vs str — wrapper returns str for API consistency.
title: Epic: orjson Adoption (P4.2)

description: Documentation improvements: architecture overview diagram (Mermaid), device type decision tree flowchart, error taxonomy reference page, and instruction file template for adopter projects.
title: Epic: Documentation Improvements (P7.x + P3.2 + P3.4)

description: Add PyPI trusted publisher attestations to the release workflow. PEP 740 defines the attestation format; pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish supports generating them via --attestations flag. Includes SLSA provenance generation using slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator or actions/attest-build-provenance. Requires id-token: write permission and PyPI trusted publisher configuration.
title: PyPI attestations (PEP 740 / SLSA provenance)

description: Update telemetry guide, ADR-014 amendment noting init= as recommended lifecycle. Configuration guide filter examples.
title: Documentation and ADR-014 amendment

description: When telemetry and command share a name, create ONE DeviceContext. Both runners use the shared context. Update _build_contexts to detect overlapping names across telemetry+command registries and produce a single context per unique name.
title: Deduplicate _build_contexts for shared names

description: Delete ~8 delegate methods (~90 lines) from _app.py: _resolve_settings(), _resolve_intervals(), _create_mqtt(), _create_services(), _install_signal_handlers(), _publish_device_availability(), _build_contexts(), _resolve_adapters(). These are dead code — _run_async() already calls _wiring.* functions directly. Verify no tests call them (they are private). Final cleanup pass for Epic 1. Acceptance: all tests pass, _app.py is shorter/cleaner.
title: P5.2: Remove delegate methods on App

description: Implement MedianFilter (VecDeque<f64> with sort for sliding window) and OneEuroFilter (adaptive cutoff algorithm) as #[pyclass]. Same test parametrization pattern as Pt1Filter. Learning: Rust ownership model with VecDeque, value semantics. Acceptance: all existing filter tests pass for both backends.
title: P4.1c: MedianFilter + OneEuroFilter in Rust

description: After the single build step, run syft to generate a CycloneDX JSON SBOM from the wheel and attach it as a GitHub Release asset.
title: Add SBOM generation to release workflow
